intro: Black and Prowler both target the security scanning space, and on paper their pricing, lock-in and ratings land close together. The useful split is positioning: Black is pitched at mobile, while Prowler is pitched at dev teams needing sast, dast, or sca in the pipeline. Beyond the headline, Black carries low lock-in (oss python formatter) and free and open-source; Prowler carries low lock-in (free oss cloud security) and free and open-source. Black is a fit when you are mobile. Prowler is a fit when you are dev teams needing sast, dast, or sca in the pipeline. The honest trade-off: Black is explicitly not for hobby projects; Prowler is explicitly not for projects with no regulatory or security compliance needs. On the product side, Black is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Python Formatter, and Prowler is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Cloud Security Posture Management.
quick_take: Black is for mobile; Prowler is for dev teams needing sast, dast, or sca in the pipeline; decide on which best-fit use case is yours.
verdict_a: Choose Black if you are mobile and comfortable with its free and open-source.
verdict_b: Choose Prowler if you are dev teams needing sast, dast, or sca in the pipeline and comfortable with its free and open-source.

faq.a: Both tools use free pricing. Black is listed as free and open-source; Prowler as free and open-source. Without specific volume and seat assumptions it is not possible to say which is cheaper in general.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Black to Prowler?
faq.a: Migration from Black to Prowler is plausible because Black has low lock-in (oss python formatter). You would still have to map config, permissions and integrations to Prowler's model. Plan for dual-run and rollback.

Both tools tie at 5/5 for developer experience on our scoring. The real difference will show up in your specific workflow, not the headline rating. A one-hour spike on both is the fastest way to decide.

faq.q: Is Prowler a good alternative to Black?
faq.a: Prowler is pitched at dev teams needing sast, dast, or sca in the pipeline, which overlaps with Black's fit (mobile) but is not identical. Note that Prowler is explicitly not for projects with no regulatory or security compliance needs, so it only replaces Black if you are outside that exclusion. Feature-for-feature parity is not guaranteed, so confirm the specific capability you rely on before switching.
